mechanism_narrative: Low federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window or positive returns occur in less than 55% of low-rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points within three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weak consumer demand, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 60% of triggered instances or t-stat for return difference versus baseline is less than 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital spending growth, generating strong positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint signal produces negative returns in more than 50% of cases or correlation with 60-day returns drops below 0.15 in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the industrial production index during a monetary tightening cycle reflects supply-side bottlenecks and rising input costs, triggering short-term negative equity returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.01 when FEDFUNDS is rising, or 5-day returns are positive in more than 65% of contraction periods.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation remains below 2.5% while the federal funds rate stays under 0.5%, markets price in prolonged accommodative policy, generating positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of triggered instances or out-of-sample Sharpe ratio falls below 0.5.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between declining nonfarm payrolls and rising unemployment indicates structural labor market weakness, forecasting negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Divergence signal yields positive returns in more than 60% of cases or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant with p-value greater than 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly increases corporate earnings expectations and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.03 over 3-year rolling windows or 60-day returns show no significant correlation with GDP growth with p-value greater than 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Expansion in industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase reflects improving supply chain efficiency and rising output, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Signal produces negative returns in more than 55% of recovery-phase instances or predictive accuracy drops below 52% in walk-forward testing.
